title: Sindhu, Shetty, Sen Crash Out of China Open Round of 16
indian_angle: India's top shuttler PV Sindhu and promising juniors Ayush Shetty and Lakshya Sen exited the China Open in the Round of 16.
summary: PV Sindhu lost to Chen Yufei after holding match points, while Ayush Shetty and Lakshya Sen also faced defeat in their respective matches at the China Open 2026.

title: PV Sindhu Wins Japan Open Title, Ends Drought
indian_angle: PV Sindhu secured a significant Japan Open title, marking a strong return to form and answering doubters with a scorching win.
summary: PV Sindhu clinched the Japan Open title with a dominant performance over home contender Akane Yamaguchi, signaling a strong comeback to the top tier of badminton.

title: BAI Adopts 3x15 Scoring System for Junior Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India introduced a new 3x15 scoring system for junior domestic tournaments, impacting young Indian players.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has implemented a new 3x15 scoring system for U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups in domestic ranking tournaments and National Championships.

title: Delhi World Championships 2026: Attracting Gen Z and Alpha Fans
indian_angle: This article discusses the importance of attracting younger audiences to the upcoming BWF World Championships 2026, which will be hosted in Delhi.
summary: With the BWF World Championships 2026 set to be held in Delhi, there's a growing focus on engaging younger generations, Gen Z and Gen Alpha, to ensure the sport's future popularity in India.

title: Player Eligibility for World Junior Championships 2026 Announced
indian_angle: This circular outlines the eligibility criteria for Indian players aiming to represent the country at the World Junior Championships 2026.
summary: Badminton Association of India has issued a circular detailing the eligibility criteria for players aspiring to be selected for the World Junior Championships 2026 in Cairo, Egypt.

title: BAI Coaches Development Program Underway in Guwahati and Raipur
indian_angle: BAI is conducting a coaches development program in Guwahati and Raipur, focusing on strengthening the grassroots badminton ecosystem in India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India is running a Coaches Development Program at the Grassroot Level in NCE Guwahati and Raipur, aimed at enhancing coaching standards across the country.
